title: Expression and purification of bacterial proteins (via  N-terminal His-tag)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"64ipj","text":"Recombinant protein production in bacteria provides an efficient system for obtaining high yields in a short time and at low cost. Although not every protein is suitable for expression in bacteria, it is usually the first choice to start expression trials. If successful, more elaborate protein purification protocols can be developed. Here we provide a two-step protocol for protein purification from bacterial cells. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"2hknm","text":"This protocol requires handling of genetically modified organisms which requires an S1 facility.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Thaw 10 µL E.coli Rosetta cells gently on ice
Next morning, dilute grown overnight culture 10 mL into 1000 mL terrific broth expression medium containing the appropriate antibiotic
From a single colony of the LB plates inoculate 50 mL, LB medium containing the appropriate selection pressure
Apply heatshock for 00:00:45, sec in a 42 °C, waterbath

Add 25 ng respective expression plasmid and keep On ice for 00:30:00, min ♦ DARPin E11 in pQE30 vector (Qiagen) - this encodes: N-terminal 8xHis-tag and a C-terminal 3xFLAG-tag ♦ Rab8 in pET28A vector (Novagen) - this encodes a N‑terminal TEV‑cleavable 6xHis-tag
Let cells recover for 00:05:00, min On ice
Add 100 µL, LB medium and incubate cells for 00:30:00, min at 37 °C
Plate cell suspension on LB agar plates containing appropriate selection pressure
Plates can be stored at 4 °C for several weeks or until further use
Once OD600 reaches 1.0, reduce temperature to 18 °C and wait for another 00:30:00, min before adding 500 millimolar (mM), IPTG
Incubate for 18:00:00, hours shaking at 180 rpm, 18 °C,
Harvest cells by centrigufation with 1000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes min
Freeze pellet and store -20 °C until further use

Thaw pellet and resuspend in 300 mL lysis buffer Lysis buffer contains 50 millimolar (mM) HEPES pH 7.4 , 500 millimolar (mM) NaCl 20 millimolar (mM) imidazole 0.5 millimolar (mM) TCEP 5 % (v/v) glycerol
Lyse by sonication 3 rounds with 5 sec pulse, 10 sec pause, 3 min total pulse time On ice
Clear lysate by ultracentrifugation 1000000 x g, 4 °C, 45 minutes
Perform Ni-NTA

Concentrate eluate to volume of 5 mL and subject to size-exclusion chromatography on a S200 gel filtration column size-exclusion buffer contains 20 millimolar (mM) HEPES pH 7.4 150 millimolar (mM) NaCl 0.5 millimolar (mM) TCEP 5 % (v/v) glycerol
Pool fractions containing your protein of interest
Measure protein concentration and do additional quality control measurements such as SDS PAGE with Coomassie staining, mass spectrometry to validate you sample
Load supernatant onto pre-equilibrated Ni-NTA sepharose beads
Wash beads rigorously with lysis buffer (50 CV)
Elute protein using lysis buffer supplemented with 300 millimolar (mM), imidazole
Flash freeze aliquots of your protein of interest and store at -80 °C
Incubate Overnight at 37 °C
Incubate the liquid culture shaking at 180 rpm, 37 °C, , overnight
Grow cells shaking at 180 rpm, 37 °C, until OD600 reached 1.0 This may take about 04:00:00, h but regularly check OD600.
creator: Verena Dederer
